channel.title: Events on BRB Robotics | FTC Team 26502 | Charlottesville, Virginia
channel.description: Recent content in Events on BRB Robotics | FTC Team 26502 | Charlottesville, Virginia

item.title: 2026 3D Printing Workshops
item.description:
Workshop Overview

Time: 1:30 PM - 2:30 PM
Dates: Sunday, March 8, 2026; Sunday, April 12, 2026; Sunday, May 10, 2026
For: Grades 3-5 (families welcome)
Where: Blue Ridge Boost: 2171 Ivy Rd, Charlottesville, VA 22903
Entry Fee: Paid registration (see sign-up buttons below)
How to Attend: Registration required (no drop-ins)

Description

Join BRB Robotics (FTC Team 26502) for a hands-on 3D Printing Workshop designed for students in grades 3–5. Participants will learn the basics of 3D printing, see how a printer works, and explore how teams use 3D printing to prototype and build parts for robotics. We’ll cover basic printer safety, common materials, and the steps from a digital design to a finished print. Space is limited, so please register in advance.

item.title: 2026 Open Practices
item.description:
Open Practice (Watch + Q&A) Overview

Time: 1:30 PM - 2:30 PM
Date: Sunday, March 1, 2026; Sunday, April 5, 2026; Sunday, May 3, 2026
For: Grades 3-5 (families welcome)
Where: 2117 Ivy Road, Charlottesville, VA
Entry Fee: Free
How to Attend: Drop-in

Open Practice (Watch + Q&A) Description

Come watch BRB Robotics during a post-season open practice. Visitors can observe our team driving the robot, testing improvements, and reviewing what worked (and what we’re changing) from the season. Feel free to ask questions as we work—we’ll explain what we’re doing, how the robot functions, and what it’s like to be on an FTC team. This is a relaxed, family-friendly event for students in grades 3–5 and their families.

item.title: FLL Explore Teams
item.description:
FLL Explore Teams — Archaeology Overview

Theme: Archaeology
When: Every Sunday starting March 29, 2026 till the competition Sunday May 24, 2026
Time: 2:30 PM – 3:30 PM
Where: 2171 Ivy Road, Charlottesville, VA
Who: Ages 7–9 (typically grades 2–4)
Teams: 3–4 teams of 3–4 students each
Capacity: 12–16 students total
Coaching: Coached by our FTC team
Showcase: May (date TBD)

What It Is

Students learn teamwork and basic engineering with LEGO-based robotics and a themed project. This season’s theme is archaeology. Our FTC team will coach and guide teams through the season.

More Info

Learn More: FIRST LEGO League

Sign Up
All funds from sign-ups will go directly to supporting FTC Team 26502, BRB Robotics.

item.title: FLL Explore Expo
FLL Explore Expo
item.description: Come check out our local FIRST Lego League (FLL) scrimmage, where teams will battle it out in an exciting robot game! Watch as they put their creativity and engineering skills to the test on the field.

item.title: Robotics Building Workshop
item.description:
FTC Robotics Camp Overview

Time: 1 pm - 5 pm
Date: 10, 17, 24 of August
Max participants: 6
Age: 7-10th graders
Entry Fee: $150

FTC Robotics Camp Schedule


Day 1: Build the Basic Drivebase

1:00 PM – 1:30 PM: Arrival and Icebreaker
1:30 PM – 2:45 PM: Building Session 1
2:45 PM – 3:00 PM: Break
3:00 PM – 4:00 PM: Build Session 2

Day 2: Test and Modify Drivebase

1:00 PM – 1:15 PM: Arrival and Recap
1:15 PM – 1:30 PM: Introduction to Testing
1:30 PM – 2:30 PM: Testing Session
2:30 PM – 2:45 PM: Break
2:45 PM – 3:30 PM: Modification Session
3:30 PM – 4:00 PM: Final Testing

Day 3: Compete on the Obstacle Course

1:00 PM – 1:15 PM: Arrival and Final Preparations
1:15 PM – 1:30 PM: Obstacle Course Overview
1:30 PM – 2:30 PM: Practice Runs
2:30 PM – 2:45 PM: Break
2:45 PM – 3:30 PM: Competition Time
3:30 PM – 4:00 PM: Wrap-Up and Celebration

All funds from workshop sign-ups will go directly to supporting FTC Team 26502, BRB Robotics.

item.title: Build, Code, Compete! Lego Robotics
item.description:
Build, Code, Compete! Lego Robotics Overview

Time: 1 pm - 4 pm
Date: 9, 16, 23 of August
Max participants: 10
Ages: 8-12
Entry Fee: $199.99

Build, Code, Compete! Lego Robotics Schedule


Day 1: Build

1:00-1:15: Arrival/Introductions
1:15-1:30: Make teams
1:30-2:45: Build Session 1
2:45-3:00: Snack/Break
3:00-4:00: Build Session 2

Day 2: Code

1:00-1:15: Arrival/Introductions
1:15-1:30: Make teams
1:30-2:45: Code Session 1
2:45-3:00: Snack/Break
3:00-4:00: Code Session 2
3:30 PM – 4:00 PM: Final Testing

Day 3 & 4: Compete

Saturday:
2:30 pm to 6:30 pm — practice and set up

Sunday:
9:00 am to 10:00 am — registration, robot inspection, and set up
10:00 am to 12:00 pm — qualification matches
12:00 pm to 1:00 pm — lunch
1:00 pm to 3:00 pm — qualification matched
3:00 pm to 4:00 pm — break
4:00 pm to 5:00 pm — closing ceremony

All funds from workshop sign-ups will go directly to supporting FTC Team 26502, BRB Robotics.
